healthy.summary: Plant looks healthy — no treatment needed.

early_blight.summary: Early blight (Alternaria solani). Remove affected leaves and apply a chlorothalonil-based fungicide on a 7–10 day schedule.

late_blight.summary: Late blight (Phytophthora infestans). Act fast — apply a copper or mancozeb fungicide and destroy heavily infected plants.

healthy.summary: Plant looks healthy — no treatment needed.

early_blight.summary: Early blight. Prune lower leaves and apply chlorothalonil or copper fungicide weekly.

late_blight.summary: Late blight. Apply mancozeb or copper fungicide immediately and remove infected foliage.

leaf_mold.summary: Leaf mold (Passalora fulva). Improve ventilation and apply chlorothalonil or mancozeb.

septoria_leaf_spot.summary: Septoria leaf spot. Remove infected leaves and apply copper or chlorothalonil fungicide.

bacterial_spot.summary: Bacterial spot. Apply copper-based bactericide; rotate crops next season.

healthy.summary: Plant looks healthy — no treatment needed.

common_rust.summary: Common rust (Puccinia sorghi). Apply a triazole fungicide such as propiconazole if infection is heavy.

northern_leaf_blight.summary: Northern leaf blight (Exserohilum turcicum). Apply azoxystrobin or propiconazole; rotate crops.

gray_leaf_spot.summary: Gray leaf spot (Cercospora zeae-maydis). Apply a strobilurin or triazole fungicide and improve residue management.
